task_objective: Eliminate the unconditional 3-5 second Ludusavi refresh on the first Quick Access Menu (QAM) open after a Deck reboot by caching the installed Steam App IDs.
design_decisions: Instead of using a generic hash, the frontend extracts the Steam App IDs using SteamClient.Apps.GetInstalledApps(), sorts them numerically, and passes them as a comma-separated string.
The backend stores this string in state.json as 'installed_app_ids'.
When refresh_games is called, if the provided installed_app_ids string matches the one in state.json, the backend instantly returns the cached game list.
If the installed_app_ids string differs, or if a manual refresh is requested, the backend performs a full ludusavi statuses refresh and updates the stored installed_app_ids.
The _refreshed_once property was completely removed from the Python backend, simplifying state management and allowing cache use on the very first boot.
results: The QAM now opens instantly even on the first use after a system reboot, relying on the robust app ID tracking to invalidate the cache only when new games are installed or removed.
